description_long: This object consists of a stack of three thick, old-fashioned books with hard covers, topped by a fourth book that is slightly separated and leaning against the stack. The covers are a rich, reddish-brown leather with visible wear, embossed geometric patterns, and two metal clasps on the topmost book. The spines are ribbed and show signs of aging, with the pages appearing yellowed and rough-edged, suggesting age and frequent use. The books are of substantial size, with the covers extending slightly beyond the pages, and the edges of the pages are uneven and textured. The overall appearance is that of antique or medieval tomes, possibly used for scholarly or archival purposes. No visible illustrations or text are present on the covers or spines, and the design is plain but detailed in its craftsmanship.
description: A stack of four large, antique leather-bound books with embossed covers, metal clasps, and aged, yellowed pages. The books are richly textured and show signs of wear.
description_view_0: Three books are stacked horizontally with a fourth book leaning against the stack, showing the embossed cover and metal clasps of the top book.
description_view_1: The view shows the flat back cover of the leaning book, with the stack of books partially visible behind it.
description_view_2: The spines and page edges of the books are visible, highlighting the ribbed spine and the rough, yellowed pages.
description_view_3: The front and spines of the stacked books are visible, with the leaning book standing upright and showing its page edges.
attribute_spine_design: The spines are ribbed and covered in brown leather, with no visible text or decorative elements other than the ribbing and natural wear.
